title: Xanthocephalum microcephalum
taxonomy: Xanthocephalum microcephalum is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Xanthocephalum, which is a group of plants native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Xanthocephalum microcephalum places it within the subfamily Asteroideae and the tribe Mutisieae. This plant is often referred to as a type of shrub or subshrub, due to its growth habit and stem characteristics. The classification of Xanthocephalum microcephalum is still being studied and refined by botanists and researchers.

Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. intro: Xanthocephalum microcephalum, also known as small-headed yellowtop or yellowtop, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Xanthocephalum microcephalum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever and colds, as well as to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation.
